novel_features.name: One-shot booking
novel_features.description: Find a slot and book it in a single command — no slot/reserve/create/confirm chain.
novel_features.rationale: Composes four API calls (slots available → reserve → create → confirm) into one safe operation with --dry-run. Every existing Cal.com tool exposes raw endpoint mirrors and forces the agent to chain them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent or operator wants to book a meeting end-to-end without managing slot reservation state by hand.

novel_features.name: Today's agenda
novel_features.description: Today's bookings with status, attendees, and meeting links — read from the local store, no API call needed.
novel_features.rationale: Joins bookings, event types, and attendee data locally. The API has no agenda endpoint; without a store you must fetch and compose every time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: First-thing-in-the-morning view that works offline and stays cheap to call repeatedly.

novel_features.name: Week view
novel_features.description: 7-day calendar view of upcoming bookings, with conflict highlighting and per-day rollup counts.
novel_features.rationale: Rendered from the local store; conflict detection cross-references external calendar busy times.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you need a one-look view of the upcoming week without paging through API responses.

novel_features.name: Cross-event-type slot search
novel_features.description: Find first available slot across multiple event types in one call, ranked by start time.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com's /slots endpoint takes a single event-type ID. We fan out, merge, and dedup so the agent can ask 'which of these event types has the earliest opening?'
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the agent doesn't care which meeting type — only when the next available slot is.

novel_features.name: Booking analytics
novel_features.description: Booking volume, density, no-show rate, and cancellation rate across a window, grouped by event type / attendee / weekday / hour.
novel_features.rationale: The API has no analytics endpoints. The local store enables aggregations that no individual API call exposes. Cal.com Insights is paid-tier only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when scoring a workflow's health, planning team capacity, or hunting cancellation patterns. `--metric no-show` and `--metric cancellations` replace separate noshow/cancellation commands.

novel_features.name: Conflict detection
novel_features.description: Detects overlaps between active Cal.com bookings and external calendar busy-times.
novel_features.rationale: Joining bookings (Cal.com) with calendar busy-times (external) requires data from two sources held together. The store has both.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Spot last-minute calendar additions that didn't propagate into Cal.com availability.

novel_features.name: Availability gap finder
novel_features.description: Finds open windows in your schedule that are available but unbooked, filtered by minimum block size.
novel_features.rationale: Correlates schedule availability with booking history in the local store. The API has no analogous filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when looking for capacity to take new meetings or to spot underused windows worth promoting.

novel_features.name: Team workload balance
novel_features.description: Booking distribution across team members over a window — surfaces overloaded vs underutilized hosts.
novel_features.rationale: Joins bookings against team membership locally. The API has no team-workload endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when tuning round-robin weights or planning team capacity.

novel_features.name: Webhook coverage
novel_features.description: Audits registered webhook triggers against the canonical set and reports lifecycle events with no subscriber.
novel_features.rationale: Comparison of registered vs canonical triggers requires both a static catalog and the registered list. No vendor tool ships this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before relying on webhooks in production to confirm every lifecycle stage has a handler.

novel_features.name: Stale event types
novel_features.description: Event types with zero bookings in the last N days — candidates for removal.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-references local bookings against the local event-type list. The API offers no analogous filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run during quarterly cleanups to retire dead booking links.

novel_features.name: Pending review
novel_features.description: Pending-confirmation bookings sorted by age, with default 24h max-age cutoff.
novel_features.rationale: API only filters by status; we add age-aware sort and `--max-age` so agents can prioritize the oldest pending first.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when sweeping the pending queue to confirm/decline before the SLA window closes.

novel_features.name: Webhook trigger catalog
novel_features.description: Static reference of every valid Cal.com webhook trigger constant, grouped by lifecycle stage.
novel_features.rationale: Cal.com publishes triggers in docs but no API endpoint enumerates them. Curated reference shipped in-CLI saves agents from guessing strings.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before writing webhook scaffolding, so trigger strings are exact.

narrative.headline: Every Cal.com feature, plus offline agendas, composed booking flows, and analytics no other Cal.com tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: A Go single-binary CLI for Cal.com v2 with a local SQLite mirror that turns scheduling data into something queryable. Composed intents like `book` collapse slot-find / reserve / create / confirm into one safe call with `--dry-run`. The store powers `today`, `week`, `analytics`, and `conflicts` — views Cal.com's own API has no equivalent for.
narrative.auth_narrative: Cal.com uses Bearer API keys (the `cal_live_*` prefix from Settings → Developer → API Keys). Set `CAL_COM_API_KEY` or run `auth set-token` once; `doctor` will tell you which source is active and whether the key reaches /v2/me. Per-endpoint `cal-api-version` headers (Bookings 2024-08-13, Slots 2024-09-04, etc.) are handled automatically.

quickstart.comment: Store your cal_live_ API key; or export CAL_COM_API_KEY
Confirm auth, reachability, and account match
Mirror bookings, event types, schedules, teams, and webhooks into the local store
View today's bookings offline; no API call after sync
Compose a full booking flow safely; drop --dry-run to execute

troubleshoots.fix: Run `doctor` — the token may be missing the `cal_live_` prefix or have been revoked. Set CAL_COM_API_KEY or `auth set-token`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Slots return empty for a busy week
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the event type's host has a default schedule active (`schedules default`) and at least one connected calendar (`calendars list`).
Run `sync` to refresh; the store is incremental and won't catch new bookings until you sync.
Run `webhooks coverage` to see if the lifecycle event you expect is registered, then `webhooks triggers` for valid trigger names.
Cal.com allows 120 req/min on API keys. Use `--limit` and the local store to reduce repeat calls.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for cal-com-pp-cli when an agent needs Cal.com data for booking, agenda views, or analytics. The local store keeps repeated calls cheap, and composed intents like `book` and `slots find` collapse multi-step API chains into one operation. For raw API parity (any v2 endpoint at all) the absorbed surface matches the official MCP server. Skip this CLI for org-wide enterprise admin flows that require OAuth managed-user delegation — those are still better served via Cal.com's web UI today.

recipes.title: Book the next available 30-min meeting
recipes.command: cal-com-pp-cli slots find --event-type-ids 96532 --start "$(date +%Y-%m-%d)" --end "$(date -v+7d +%Y-%m-%d)" --first-only --json | jq -r '.start' | xargs -I{} cal-com-pp-cli book --event-type-id 96532 --start {} --attendee-name "X" --attendee-email "x@y.com"
recipes.explanation: Combine `slots find` and `book` to grab the first opening across the next week.

recipes.title: Today's agenda with only the fields agents care about
recipes.explanation: `--agent` produces a compact JSON shape; `--select` narrows it to four fields, keeping context cheap on deeply-nested booking responses.

recipes.title: Find no-show patterns over the last quarter
recipes.explanation: Identify attendees with chronic no-show problems before they damage team capacity.

recipes.title: Audit webhook coverage before going to production
recipes.explanation: Lists registered triggers vs canonical set; flags lifecycle stages without a subscriber.

recipes.title: Cancel a booking with a reason in dry-run first
recipes.command: cal-com-pp-cli bookings cancel <uid> --reason "Customer requested" --dry-run && cal-com-pp-cli bookings cancel <uid> --reason "Customer requested"
recipes.explanation: Dry-run shows the exact request body, then the same command executed for real.

trigger_phrases: what's on my cal.com agenda today
